title: Unvalidated User Input
description: The system’s design doesn’t explicitly state how user input is validated. This creates an opportunity for attackers to inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, Cross-Site Scripting) or manipulate data by submitting crafted input.  Without proper input sanitization and validation, the system is vulnerable to data corruption and potential compromise.
attack_vector: Submission of malicious input through forms, URLs, or API requests designed to exploit vulnerabilities in the application’s code.
mitigations: Implement strict input validation and sanitization on all user-supplied data.
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to filter malicious requests.
